extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE Arnaud won the sprint by a bike length after LUTSENKO Alexey's late attack was caught in the final 31km
tactical_patterns: LUTSENKO Alexey attacking solo from breakaway with under 31km remaining signals Astana's willingness to sacrifice GC position for stage hunting.
Lotto-Soudal and Direct Energy jointly controlled break margins after Stage 1's losses, learning peloton split risk from previous day.
BROWN Nathan targeting virtual yellow from 31st GC shows Cannondale prioritizing early leader jersey over sprint support in week-long stage races.

rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: sixth win of 2017 and beating Kristoff/Bouhanni one month pre-Tour signals peak form timing strategy working
rider_signals.BOUHANNI Nacer: positive: third place one month after Yorkshire crash shows rapid form recovery trajectory
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: second to Démare on slightly ascending 4600m finishing straight reveals climb-to-sprint transition weakness
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: neutral: solo attack from break with under 31km shows high risk tolerance but poor timing against organized sprint teams
rider_signals.BROWN Nathan: neutral: targeting virtual yellow from 31st GC demonstrates aggressive breakaway selection but team lacked commitment to defend
